finding: A same-navigation binding is insufficient across persisted history restoration because the same Document can be shown again. Event Timing replay and coverage evidence must be isolated in a newly rotated opaque evidence epoch after pageshow.persisted is true.

primary_sources.title: HTML Living Standard — PageTransitionEvent.persisted
primary_sources.observed: For pageshow, persisted is false when the page is newly loaded and true otherwise.

primary_sources.title: Navigation Timing Level 2
primary_sources.observed: The navigation type back_forward denotes navigation applied from history.

design_implication: Collectors should rotate an opaque evidenceEpochId and clear replay and coverage accumulators on a persisted pageshow before interpretation resumes. A persisted restore is rejected unless the prior epoch is present, reset is observed, and the new epoch differs.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical files before commit

uncertainty: No deployed collector currently emits or rotates evidenceEpochId.
No physical Safari back-forward-cache packet has been evaluated.
Navigation Timing Level 2 remains a Working Draft and may change.
falsification_route: Show a conforming persisted pageshow where combining pre-freeze and post-restore Event Timing evidence cannot alter interpretation.
Show that a valid rotated epoch is rejected by the validator.
Run the integrated interpretation suite and identify a regression caused by epoch isolation.
